description: Instructional text appears on the screen: 'Great. To start, please click the "Share" button'
frame_description: A dark screen with white instructional text appearing at the bottom.

description: Task instructions are displayed progressively on the screen, detailing a travel planning scenario from the Sunshine Coast, Queensland to London.
frame_description: White text updates on a dark background, providing context for a user research task.

description: User hovers over the 'Open' button that appeared at the end of the instructions.
frame_description: The cursor is positioned over a circular 'Open' button that has just appeared on the screen.

description: Task instructions are progressively displayed and updated on a dark screen, including a reminder to think aloud and the specific task scenario.
frame_description: Dark screen with white text updating with instructions.

description: Scroll E (magnitude: 4.5, uniformity: 0.63)
The cursor hovers over the 'Repeat the task instructions' button.
The cursor hovers over the 'Open' button.
User clicks the 'Open' button to proceed to the task.
The page navigates from the instruction screen to the Google homepage.
User types 'sky' into the Google search input field.
User is reading the initial task instructions about planning a trip to London.
User hovers over the 'Open' button while reading the instructions.
The instruction text updates to a new prompt: 'Yes, please think aloud as you'.
User pauses to read the updated prompt.
The instruction text updates further to: 'Yes, please think aloud as you go. What are you looking at right now?'.
User hovers over the 'Open' button.
User hovers over the 'Repeat the task instructions' button.
User types 'sunsh' into the 'From' origin airport input field, replacing 'Sydney (SYD)'
frame_description: The user updates the origin airport in the search form, triggering a location suggestion dropdown.

description: User waits while the flight search results are loading, with the cursor resting near the bottom right of the screen.
frame_description: A loading screen for flight results showing skeleton loaders and a 'Searching' indicator.

description: User selects the '2+ stops' filter checkbox in the sidebar to filter flight results.
frame_description: The user interacts with the sidebar filters while flight results begin to populate.

description: Flight search results populate the page, replacing the skeleton loaders and updating the provider count.
frame_description: The UI transitions from a loading state to displaying specific flight options and prices.

description: The flight search results finished loading, transitioning from 'Checked 25 of 26 providers' to '997 results sorted by Best'.
frame_description: The loading progress bar and provider count disappear, replaced by the total number of results and the sort order.

description: User clicked the 'Book now' button to proceed with the flight booking.
frame_description: The user interacts with the primary 'Book now' call-to-action button on a flight search result.

description: User hovers over the 'Book Direct with Virgin Australia' promotional link
frame_description: The cursor moves over the Virgin Australia booking option, changing from an arrow to a pointer.

description: The page scrolls up to reveal the flight category summary tabs (Best, Cheapest, Fastest)
frame_description: The view shifts from a specific flight result to the top-level summary tabs.

description: User hovers over the 'Best' flight summary tab showing a price of $2,693
frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the 'Best' flight option tab, indicated by the pointer icon.

description: User types into the destination search field, updating the text from 'lo' to 'lon'.
frame_description: The destination input field is focused and shows the text 'lon'.

description: User selects 'London, Greater London, United Kingdom' from the destination autocomplete suggestions, updating the field from 'london'.
frame_description: The destination input field now contains the full formatted address 'London, Greater London, United Kingdom'.
